text:
Traffic forecasting is a fundamental problem in multivariate time series analysis and a first-class research problem in the field of AI. Main challenges include handling the heterogeneous, non-stationary, and spatial-temporal complexities of traffic data. In this work, we present a new Graph Structure Learning (GSL) approach called Spatio-Temporal Meta-Graph Learning (ST-MGL) to tackle the highly complex spatio-temporal heterogeneity and non-stationarity of traffic patterns.

We incorporate the proposed framework into the Meta-Graph Convolutional Recurrent Network (MegaCRN). Specifically, MegaCRN leverages a novel component called the Meta-Graph Learner comprised of a “Meta-Node Bank” to store learnt prototypes of traffic patterns within the context of the traditional Graph Convolutional Recurrent Network (GCRN) encoder-decoder architecture. Experiments were conducted on widely used benchmarks (METR-LA and PEMS-BAY) and a novel, large-scale real-world traffic dataset EXPY-TKY is introduced, which represents traffic speed on the links of the entire Tokyo expressway network and comprises of 1843 data points.

We conduct experiments using large-scale traffic datasets and show that our proposed MegaCRN model is significantly better than state-of-the-art models in all cases. We study the learned components and confirm that MegaCRN extracts independent patterns from the raw data in both spatial and temporal domains. Our proposed model can easily learn the patterns in both normal and routine traffic conditions as well as unusual traffic scenarios like accidents. Our experimental code and large-scale traffic datasets are available at https://github.com/deepkashiwa20/MegaCRN.
